tool_args.label: Select Bus Service
tool_args.raw_text: Select Bus Service (SBS) is New York City's brand name for Bus Rapid Transit

payload.description: New York City's brand name for Bus Rapid Transit

evidence_refs.source_quote: Select Bus Service (SBS) is New York City's brand name for Bus Rapid Transit: an improved bus service that offers fast, frequent, and reliable service on high-ridership bus routes.

tool_args.raw_text: Bx6 South Bronx Crosstown Select Bus Service

payload.project_name: Bx6 South Bronx Crosstown Select Bus Service
payload.description: Proposed Select Bus Service on the Bx6 South Bronx corridor, serving nearly 25,000 daily riders, connecting to subway lines and Metro-North

evidence_refs.source_quote: Bx6 South Bronx Crosstown Select Bus Service

• Serves nearly 25,000 daily riders • Connects to 1 , C , 4 , B , D , 2 , 5 , 6 subway lines, Metro-North, and 20 bus routes, including Bx41 SBS • 76% of households within a quarter-mile of the route do not own a vehicle

payload.description: Existing local bus route from Washington Heights (W 155 St and Broadway) to Hunts Point

evidence_refs.source_quote: The map illustrates the existing Bx6 local bus route, highlighted in a thick blue line. The route begins at Washington Heights, near the intersection of W 155 St and Broadway, and proceeds east through the Bronx.

payload.description: Proposed Select Bus Service variant of the Bx6, with stops only at SBS stops

evidence_refs.source_quote: The diagram illustrates the proposed service plan branching from the 'Existing Bx6' service. It splits into two options: 'Bx6 SBS' and 'Bx6 local'. Existing Bx6 (Grey box) branches into: Bx6 SBS (Dark Blue box): Stops only at SBS stops. Bx6 local (Light Blue box): Stops at every stop.

The Bx6 South Bronx corridor was identified as a potential candidate for Select Bus Service in the 2009 Bus Rapid Transit Phase II Study.
The route begins at Washington Heights, near the intersection of W 155 St and Broadway, and proceeds east through the Bronx.
